goal: Destructive tool calls are gated by a HARD enforced confirmation (not prose), with undo where the platform allows and a principal-scoped auditable record.
why: Today destructive side-effects run immediately inside the tool loop guarded only by system-prompt prose; a prompt-injected document or email could steer the model to delete OneDrive files or send company mail with no human in the loop. Stage-and-confirm makes the confirmation structural (ADR-003).
success_criteria: AC1 — executeTool for any destructive tool returns confirmation_required and calls NO adapter side-effect
AC2 — Staging persists a principal-scoped pending_actions row (status pending)
AC3 — POST /api/actions/confirm runs executeConfirmedAction, sets confirmed, records resolved_at/result
AC4 — All /api/actions routes 401 without a session; principal isolation prevents cross-principal confirm/cancel/undo
AC5 — Undo reverses move_item/rename_item via updateItem from undo_data; send_email reports no-undo
AC6 — Confirming an already-resolved action does not re-execute
AC7 — Chat surfaces pending actions with Confirm/Cancel (and Undo after reversible confirm), token-driven, loading/error/empty states, passes slop-detect
AC8 — npx tsc --noEmit 0; npx vitest run passes

tasks.title: Migration: pending_actions table
acceptance_criteria: public.pending_actions table with id, principal (check Wency/Jeanette), tool, args jsonb, summary, status (check pending/confirmed/cancelled/undone/failed), undo_data jsonb, result jsonb, created_at, resolved_at
RLS enabled with NO policies (service-role only), matching 0009_memory_facts.sql
Index idx_pending_actions_principal_status_created on (principal, status, created_at)
tasks.action: Mirror 0009_memory_facts.sql structure exactly (table -> comment -> indexes -> enable row level security). Copy the principal check verbatim. No create policy statements. status enum check lists all five states. args/undo_data/result are jsonb.

tasks.title: Stage destructive tools + extract executeConfirmedAction
acceptance_criteria: executeTool for delete_item/send_email/move_item/rename_item/schedule_email stages a pending_actions row (principal from session) and returns {status:'confirmation_required', action_id, summary}; no adapter side-effect runs
Destructive tool with no sessionPrincipal returns {error:'no verified principal in session'} and stages nothing (fail-closed)
create_folder and read-only tools unchanged (still execute inline)
executeConfirmedAction captures prior parentId/name for move/rename undo_data before updateItem; send_email is undoable:false; schedule_email stores queued id
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0
tasks.action: Add parentId?:string to DriveItem (types.ts), map raw.parentReference?.id (onedrive.ts). Create pendingActions.ts (stage/list/get/confirm/cancel/undo) over supabaseAdmin pending_actions, mirroring scheduled.ts; confirm/cancel/undo principal-scoped + status-guarded. Create executeConfirmedAction.ts with the moved move/rename/delete/send/schedule bodies capturing undo_data. In executeTool gate the DESTRUCTIVE set: fail-closed without principal, stagePendingAction, return confirmation_required.

tasks.title: /api/actions endpoints (list/confirm/cancel/undo)
acceptance_criteria: All four routes 401 when getPrincipal is null (mirror chat/route.ts)
GET /api/actions returns only the session principal's pending rows
POST /api/actions/confirm runs confirmAction -> executeConfirmedAction, records result/resolved_at/status confirmed; re-confirm of a resolved id is a no-op (409)
POST /api/actions/cancel sets cancelled, no side-effect; POST /api/actions/undo reverses reversible confirmed actions; send_email reports no-undo
Acting on another principal's id returns 404
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0
tasks.action: Each route: runtime nodejs + dynamic force-dynamic; getPrincipal guard -> 401; POST routes parse {id} with try/catch -> 400; call the matching pendingActions function with (id, principal); null/0-row -> 404; already-resolved confirm -> 409; side-effect throw -> 502. Use NextRequest/NextResponse, getPrincipal from @/lib/auth/session.

tasks.title: Chat pending-action card (Confirm/Cancel/Undo)
acceptance_criteria: After model reply and on identity mount, chat fetches GET /api/actions and renders a card per pending action with summary + Confirm + Cancel
Confirm -> POST confirm; on success shows confirmed state, Undo button for reversible tools; send_email shows sent-cannot-undo
Cancel -> POST cancel; card disappears. Undo -> POST undo; undone/error states
Card has loading/error/empty states; uses OKLCH tokens (no raw hex); SSE parse loop unchanged
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0
tasks.action: Add pending + actionBusy state; loadPending() fetches /api/actions at end of send() and in identity useEffect; confirm/cancel/undo handlers POST and reload. Render card list after .thread, before composer, reusing .btn/.btn.ghost/.btn.danger/spinner. Add .action-card styles in globals.css with existing tokens. Do not touch the streaming reader.

tasks.title: System prompt update + seam/route tests
acceptance_criteria: System prompt no longer says wait-for-confirmation-then-call; states destructive tools are auto-staged for confirmation, model proposes/calls once/relays summary, does not re-call after yes
Seam test: executeTool delete_item returns confirmation_required, mocked deleteItem NOT called, stagePendingAction called with principal Wency
Route test for confirm: 401 without principal, 404 cross-principal, confirm runs executeConfirmedAction sets confirmed, second confirm no-op
npx vitest run passes; npx tsc --noEmit exits 0
tasks.action: Rewrite client.ts system-prompt items 4/5b/6 to staged-for-confirmation language. Add staging assertions to onedriveTools.test.ts (mock pendingActions). Create confirm route.test.ts mocking getPrincipal + pendingActions, fake NextRequest, asserting the four cases.
